hook: You already know that doubling a quantity repeatedly leads to explosive growth — think of money in a savings account or a viral video racking up views. But the tools you have so far can't precisely describe *how fast* that growth happens or *when* a quantity will reach a certain level. This unit gives you exactly those tools, unlocking the mathematics behind everything from compound interest to earthquake intensity to the spread of populations.
concept: Every topic in this unit revolves around the relationship between exponential and logarithmic functions — two sides of the same coin that together let you model and solve problems where quantities grow or decay multiplicatively, formally captured by the inverse relationship $y = b^x \Leftrightarrow x = \log_b y$.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to recognize, graph, and interpret exponential functions of the form $f(x) = b^x$, understanding how the base $b$ controls growth versus decay.
Master the special base $e \approx 2.718$ and discover why the natural exponential function $f(x) = e^x$ appears throughout science, finance, and engineering.
You will learn to define logarithms as inverses of exponential functions, convert between exponential and logarithmic forms, and graph logarithmic functions with confidence.
Apply the product, quotient, and power rules of logarithms to expand and condense logarithmic expressions, building the algebraic fluency you need for equation solving.
You will solve exponential and logarithmic equations by strategically applying inverse operations and logarithm properties to isolate the unknown variable.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Modeling with Exponential and Logarithmic Functions
topic_roadmap.description: Apply exponential growth, exponential decay, and logarithmic models to real-world situations such as compound interest, radioactive half-life, and the Richter scale.
You will analyze logistic functions to model populations that grow exponentially at first but level off at a carrying capacity, capturing realistic biological and social dynamics.
